article_title: Dominion Energy's 2.6 GW Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind Farm Delivers First Power to the Grid After Surviving Federal Stop-Work Order

content_preview.title: Dominion Energy's 2.6 GW Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind Farm Delivers First Power to the Grid After Surviving Federal Stop-Work Order
content_preview.summary: The 2.6 GW Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, America's largest offshore wind farm, delivered first power on March 23 after overcoming a federal stop-work order that added $230 million in costs to the $11.5 billion project.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, known as CVOW, delivered its first electricity to the regional power grid on March 23, 2026, marking a milestone for the largest offshore wind farm in the United States. The 2.6 gigawatt facility, located 27 to 44 miles off the coast of Virginia Beach, reached the achievement after more than a decade of development and a protracted legal battle with the federal government over a stop-work order issued in late 2025.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong energy news covering milestone, legal context, and broader industry dynamics.
editor_notes.source_verification: CleanTechnica (fetched): Confirms March 23 first power, 2.6 GW, $11.5B, stop-work order, ~$230M added costs, TotalEnergies lease cancellation. Offshore Wind (fetched): Confirms March 23, CEO Robert Blue quote, 176 Siemens Gamesa 14MW turbines, Charybdis Jones Act vessel, early 2027 completion.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across both sources. Minor: article says '$228M' while CleanTechnica says '~$230M' — within reasonable rounding.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Reports political context without editorializing.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced energy news. Ready for publication.
